# sealed

analysis —
law entropy leaves through error handling — a swallowed (empty catch) or defaulted (.catch(() => x)) error hides the truth, so the entropy escapes instead of propagating. The corpus is sealed only when errors propagate; coverage is the fraction of catches that do not leak (an upper bound — real leaks ≥ detected). It is the same sin as a defined default: an assumption that leaks entropy.
